leading-[1.7]"><br></p><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]"><strong>Cardinalities — why these values:</strong></p><ul class="[li_&amp;]:mb-0 [li_&amp;]:mt-1 [li_&amp;]:gap-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-disc flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3"><li class="whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">User (1,1) — writes — (0,N) Recipe</code> → A user must have exactly one identity (1,1 on the User side), and can write zero or many recipes (0,N on the Recipe side). A recipe without an author makes no semantic sense, hence 1,1 on the Recipe side reading back.</li><br><li class="whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">Recipe (0,N) — belongs to — (1,1) Category</code> → A category can group zero or many recipes (think a freshly created "Drinks" category with no recipe yet = 0,N). Each recipe belongs to exactly one category — no nulls, no multi-category (1,1). This enforces the <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">NOT NULL</code> constraint on the FK in SQL.</li></ul><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]"><strong>What this translates to in SQL (MLD):</strong></p><ul class="[li_&amp;]:mb-0 [li_&amp;]:mt-1 [li_&amp;]:gap-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-disc flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3"><li class="whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">recipe.id_user</code> → FK referencing <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">user(id_user)</code>, <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">NOT NULL</code></li><br><li class="whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">recipe.id_category</code> → FK referencing <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">category(id_category)</code>, <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">NOT NULL</code></li><br><li class="whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Both use <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">ON DELETE</code> rules you'll need to decide on (e.g. <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">RESTRICT</code> or <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">CASCADE</code>)</li></ul><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]"><b>This is a clean 1-N model — no junction table needed, both associations resolve as simple foreign keys directly on the <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">recipe</code> table.</b></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="390" width="780" x="30" y="640" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

ul.class: [li_&]:mb-0 [li_&]:mt-1 [li_&]:gap-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-disc flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3
ul.li: User (1,1) — writes — (0,N) Recipe → A user must have exactly one identity (1,1 on the User side), and can write zero or many recipes (0,N on the Recipe side). A recipe without an author makes no semantic sense, hence 1,1 on the Recipe side reading back.

Recipe (0,N) — belongs to — (1,1) Category → A category can group zero or many recipes (think a freshly created "Drinks" category with no recipe yet = 0,N). Each recipe belongs to exactly one category — no nulls, no multi-category (1,1). This enforces the NOT NULL constraint on the FK in SQL.

p.strong: What this translates to in SQL (MLD):

ul.class: [li_&]:mb-0 [li_&]:mt-1 [li_&]:gap-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-disc flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3
ul.li: recipe.id_user → FK referencing user(id_user), NOT NULL
recipe.id_category → FK referencing category(id_category), NOT NULL
Both use ON DELETE rules you'll need to decide on (e.g. RESTRICT or CASCADE)

p.b: This is a clean 1-N model — no junction table needed, both associations resolve as simple foreign keys directly on the recipe table.

a.text: Text is not SVG - cannot display
